channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: IAF Plans Air-Dropped Swarm Drone System With 500 km Range for C-17, C-130 and C-295
item.description: India is moving toward a new class of air-launched swarm strike capability with the development of an Air Dropped Canisterised Swarm (ADC-S) system, designed for deployment from transport aircraft such as the C-17 Globemaster III, C-130J Super Hercules, and C-295. The program, being pursued under the Make-II category of the Defence Acquisition Procedure (DAP) 2020, aims to deliver a cost-effective yet highly flexible precision strike solution tailored for modern contested battlefields.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Ambitious $7 Billion Plan for 6th Generation AMCA Engine Co-Development
item.description: India’s Ministry of Defence (MoD) is poised to make a transformative investment in its aerospace ambitions, allocating approximately $7 billion for the co-development of a 6th generation jet engine to power the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) Mk-2, according to sources cited by the Indian Defence Research Wing (idrw.org).
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Project 77 SSN Program: First Keel Laying Expected by 2028 as Navy Advances Indigenous Nuclear Attack Submarine Fleet
item.description: India’s ambitious nuclear attack submarine program Project 75 Alpha (P-75A), now increasingly referred to as Project 77 is gradually moving toward its next major milestone, with the first keel laying expected around 2028 at the Shipbuilding Centre (SBC) in Visakhapatnam, according to information obtained by idrw.org from sources familiar with the program.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: MoD Issues RFI for Next-Generation Air Defence Guns (ADG-NG) and Ammunition
item.description: The Ministry of Defence (MoD), Government of India, has issued a Request for Information (RFI) for the procurement of Air Defence Guns (New Generation) (ADG-NG) along with associated ammunition, marking a fresh push to modernize the Indian Army’s air defence capabilities against evolving aerial threats.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL Rules Out ‘Shahed-Class’ Loitering Munitions, Focus Shifts to CATS Warrior UCAV
item.description: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) has clarified its strategic direction in the rapidly evolving unmanned systems domain, confirming that it will not pursue the development of Shahed-class loitering munitions. Instead, the state-owned aerospace major will concentrate on higher-end unmanned combat aerial vehicles (UCAVs), while leaving the loitering munition segment to startups and private industry.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Brayton Cycle Tech Could Transform S5-Class Submarines and Naval Power
item.description: The Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bangalore has achieved a major technological milestone with the development of a 150 kW supercritical carbon dioxide (sCO?) Brayton Cycle test loop, which is being described as the world’s first such system integrated with a solar heat source. Developed by the Interdisciplinary Centre for Energy Research in 2018, the facility is not only a breakthrough in clean energy generation but is increasingly being viewed as a foundational technology with far-reaching military implications, particularly for naval propulsion.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: South Korea Rolls Out First Production KF-21 Boramae Fighter Jet as India’s Tejas Mk2 Prototype Nears Maiden Flight
item.description: South Korea has achieved a significant milestone in its indigenous fighter programme with the rollout of the first serial production KF-21 Boramae fighter jet from the Korea Aerospace Industries (KAI) factory in Sacheon. The two-seat aircraft, bearing serial number 26-001, was unveiled in a high-profile ceremony attended by South Korean President Lee Jae Myung, marking the transition from development to full-scale production. Delivery to the Republic of Korea Air Force is expected in the second half of 2026.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pakistan’s response to any attack would be ‘swift, calibrated and decisive’: Khawaja Asif on Rajnath Singh’s remarks
item.description: Pakistan’s Defence Minister Khawaja Asif warned on Thursday (April 2, 2026) that his country’s response to any attack would be “swift, calibrated, and decisive”. Mr. Asif took to X to respond to remarks by his Indian counterpart Rajnath Singh, who earlier said that any “misadventure” from India’s neighbour in the prevailing situation would invite an “unprecedented and decisive” action.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Rajnath Singh Lays Foundation Stone Of Tunnel In Visakhapatnam To Boost Naval Research
item.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh on Friday laid the foundation stone of a state-of-the-art Large Cavitation Tunnel (LCT) facility at the Naval Science and Technological Laboratory (NSTL) of the DRDO in Visakhapatnam, an official said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Navy ensures safety of Indian commercial ships, oil tankers: Rajnath Singh
item.description: Pointing out that 95 per cent of the country’s trade, including energy supplies, is conducted through sea, Defence Minister Rajnath Singh on Friday noted the Indian Navy’s indispensable role in securing commercial shipping lanes and oil tankers against emerging maritime threats.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Crew member of US fighter jet downed over Iran rescued, CBS News reports
item.description: A crew member ?from ?a U.S. ?fighter jet ?that ?was ?shot ?down over Iran on Friday (April 3, 2026) ?has ?been rescued by ?U.S. forces. Axios and CBS News, citing unidentified sources, reported that one of the two crew on the plane had already been rescued by US special forces but that the search was ongoing for the second crew member.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: INS Taragiri Commissioned; Defence Minister Hails It As Symbol Of India’s Growing Naval Strength
item.description: The Indian Navy commissioned its stealth frigate INS Taragiri on Friday at a grand ceremony, attended by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, marking a defining moment in India’s maritime security and sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: PoK will ‘reintegrate with India soon’, says head of All India Imam Organisation
item.description: Chief Imam of the All India Imam Organisation, Umer Ahmed Ilyasi, on Friday said Pakistan-Occupied Kashmir (PoK) would “very soon” be reintegrated with India. “I want to say one thing to our neighbouring country from standing here in Kashmir that PoK, which is an integral part of India, will definitely rejoin India because the people of PoK want to have a referendum there,” Ilyasi told reporters here.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Matters To World; We Must Be More Careful, Measured In Our Responses: Syed Akbaruddin
item.description: India’s former Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Syed Akbaruddin, said that the country’s foreign policy is increasingly reflecting the aspirations of its young population and the everyday concerns of ordinary citizens, and while India matters to the world, the reverse is also true and and therefore, it must adopt a more careful and measured approach in its responses.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Family moves HC for return of mortal remains of seafarer killed in Iran war
item.description: The family of seafarer Dixit Solanki who died last month in a suspected attack on a merchant vessel off the coast of Oman has moved the Bombay High Court, seeking the return of his mortal remains. The petition, filed by Solanki’s father Amratlal Solanki and sister Mitali Solanki through advocates S B Talekar and Madhvi Ayyappan, has sought directions to the Union government to expedite the repatriation of his remains, claiming lack of clarity from the authorities.
item.dc:creator: admin
